---
title: "Clustering"
author: "alexander-gysin (alexander.gysin@usz.ch)"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup_boxes, include=FALSE}
source(here::here("code", "00_config.R"))
```

# Setup and Import
`r what_this_does("Loaded required packages including UMAP for non-linear dimensionality reduction and gtsummary for clinical cross-referencing.", "Imported the pre-cleaned clinical spine and Olink proteomics matrix.", "Sourced custom omics engines")`

```{r setup_import, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(here)
library(umap)       # Non-linear reduction
library(cluster)    # Diagnostics
library(dbscan)     # HDBSCAN Density clustering
library(igraph)     # Louvain Graph clustering
library(gtsummary)  # Clinical tables$
library(msigdbr)

# Knitr Config
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

# Output paths
current_file <- "03c_proteomics_clustering"
output_dir_data <- here::here("output", current_file)
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_data)) dir.create(output_dir_data, recursive = TRUE)
assets_dir <- here::here("docs", "assets", current_file)

# Global DEA Path
proteomics_dea_path <- here::here("output", "03b_proteomics_differential")

# Source Engines
source(here::here("code", "01_general_engines.R"))
source(here::here("code", "02_proteomics_engines.R"))

# Load Pre-Cleaned Data
master_spine <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01b_clinical_feature_engineering", "curated_clinical_spine.rds"))
data_dictionary <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01b_clinical_feature_engineering", "clinical_data_dictionary.rds"))
olink_mat <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01a_data_import_and_qc", "clean_olink_matrix.rds"))

# Fetch multiple GSEA databases into a named list
pathway_dbs <- list(
  "Hallmark" = msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "H") %>% select(gs_name, gene_symbol),
  "GO_BP" = msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "C5", subcategory = "BP") %>% select(gs_name, gene_symbol),
  "Immunologic_Signature" = msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "C7") %>% select(gs_name, gene_symbol),
  "Reactome" = msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "C2", subcategory = "CP:REACTOME") %>% select(gs_name, gene_symbol)
)
# Dynamic Feature Count
total_features <- nrow(olink_mat)
```

# Configurations
`r what_this_does("Configured the Matrix Factory, Algorithm Optimization, Clustering, and dual Phenotyping (Clinical & Feature) parameters.")`
## Clustering
```{r config}
# 0. Data Dictionary Shield & Base Excludes
unstable_vars <- data_dictionary %>% filter(grepl("ZERO VARIANCE", toupper(QC_Flag))) %>% pull(Variable)
base_excludes <- c("inCentraxx", "SELECTED", "Subject_ID", "Birth_Year", "Raynaud VAS Unknown", "Face", "Chest", "Abdomen", "Left_Upper Arms", "Right_Upper Arms", "Left_Forearms", "Right_Forearms", "Left_Hands", "Right_Hands", "Left_Fingers", "Right_Fingers", "Left_Thighs", "Right_Thighs", "Left_Legs", "Right_Legs", "Left_Feet", "Right_Feet", "Active_our", "Geschlecht", "Entnahmedatum", "Entnahmedatum_Clean", "Distance_in_m")
final_excludes <- unique(c(base_excludes, unstable_vars))

# 1. Matrix Factory Configurations
# Switch back to Active vs. Control?
matrix_prep_configs <- list(
  mat_avc_age_residualized = list(
    subset_var = NULL,
    base_features = list(
      method = "dea", dea_path = proteomics_dea_path, source_file = "ssc_vs_control.rds", 
      fdr_cutoff = 0.05, fc_cutoff = 0.58, min_features = 30, top_n = 200 
    ),
    transform = list(method = "residualize", covariates = c("Age"))
  )
  
  # mat_full_age_res = list(
  #   subset_var = NULL, # Change to NULL if you want to include Controls too
  #   base_features = list(method = "variance", top_n = total_features), 
  #   transform = list(method = "residualize", covariates = c("Age"))
  # )
)

# 2. PCA
pca_configs <- list(
  ssc_pca = list(
    input_matrix = "mat_avc_age_residualized",
    title = "SSc vs. Ctrl DEA subset PCA",
    color_vars = c("cohort_group", "Sex", "Age"),
    top_dim = 5
  )
)

# 3. Pre-Flight Algorithm Optimization Configs
# hdbscan = list(run=TRUE, minPts_range = 2:15),
k_opt_configs <- list(
  opt_SvC_DEA_PCA = list(
    title = "Optimization: Age Regressed SvC DEA PCA",
    input_matrix = "ssc_pca_top_dims", 
    k_range = 2:8,
    gap_bootstraps = 0, 
    algorithms = list(
      pam = list(run=TRUE, metric="euclidean"),
      kmeans = list(run=TRUE, nstart=25),
      hclust = list(run=TRUE, distance="euclidean", method="ward.D2"),
      louvain = list(run=TRUE, k_neighbors = 4, resolution_range = seq(0.1, 2.0, by=0.1))
    )
  )
)

# 4. Clustering Configs
# hdbscan = list(run=TRUE, minPts=3),  
clustering_configs <- list(
  job_svc = list(
    title = "Age Regressed SvC DEA PCA Clustering",
    input_matrix = "ssc_pca_top_dims", # <--- UPDATE THIS 
    k = 2, 
    n_neighbors = 10,
    color_vars = c("Age", "Sex", "cohort_group"),
    algorithms = list(
      kmeans = list(run=TRUE, nstart=25), 
      pam = list(run=TRUE, metric="euclidean"), 
      hclust = list(run=TRUE, distance="euclidean", method="ward.D2"), 
      louvain = list(run=TRUE, k_neighbors=4, resolution=0.1) 
    )
  )
)

# 5. Diagnostic Targets
diagnostic_configs <- list(
  evaluate_jobs = c("job_svc"),
  correlate_vars = c("cohort_group", "Age", "Sex")
)

```

## Phenotyping
```{r conf_pheno}
# 6. Clinical Phenotyping Targets ---------
# , "hclust","louvain"
#Note: For the value argument, I used %in% under the hood instead of ==, so you can even pass multiple values like value = c("Nonactive", "Mildly Active") if you ever need to group ordinal tiers together!
clin_vars_to_highlight <- list(
  "SSc clinical parameters" = ssc_clinical_parameters,
  "Medication" = clinical_domains$Medications
)

clin_vars_to_include <- list(
  "Exploratory" = c(clinical_domains$Demographics_and_Scores, clinical_domains$Pulmonary, clinical_domains$Cardiac, clinical_domains$Labs_and_Biomarkers, clinical_domains$Cutaneous_and_Vascular, clinical_domains$Musculoskeletal, clinical_domains$Gastrointestinal) 
)

pheno_configs <- list(
  pheno_svc = list(
    target_job = "job_svc",
    title = "SSc Only",
    target_algorithms = c("kmeans"),
        subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Active", "Non-Active")),
    variables = clin_vars_to_include,
    highlight_variables = clin_vars_to_highlight ,
    p_cutoff = 0.05,
    must_include_vars = c("cohort_group")
  ),
  
    pheno_nvn = list(
    target_job = "job_svc",
    title = "Nonactive Only",
    target_algorithms = c("kmeans"),
        subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Non-Active")),
    variables = clin_vars_to_include,
    highlight_variables = clin_vars_to_highlight ,
    p_cutoff = 0.05,
    must_include_vars = c("cohort_group")
  )


)


# 7.a Feature Phenotyping Targets (DEA) ------
feature_configs <- list(
  feat_svc = list(
    target_job = "job_svc",
    title = "SSc Only",
    input_matrix = "raw", 
    covariates = c("Age"),
    # Algorithms are now the primary key to enable contrast-per-algorithm
    algorithms = list(
      kmeans = list(
        contrasts = list(
          c1_vs_c2 = list(title = "Cluster 1 vs Cluster 2 Ssc only", desc = "Both mixed active and nonacctive", 
                          target=c("Cluster 2"), ref=c("Cluster 1"),
                          subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Active", "Non-Active")))
        )
      )
    ),
    dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,
    dea_fc_cutoff = 0.58
  ),
  
  feat_nvn = list(
    target_job = "job_svc",
    title = "Nonactive Only",
    input_matrix = "raw", 
    covariates = c("Age"),
    # Algorithms are now the primary key to enable contrast-per-algorithm
    algorithms = list(
      kmeans = list(
        contrasts = list(
          c1_vs_c2 = list(title = "Cluster 1 vs Cluster 2 Nonactive only", desc = "", 
                          target=c("Cluster 2"), ref=c("Cluster 1"),
                          subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Non-Active")))
        )
      )
    ),
    dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,
    dea_fc_cutoff = 0.58
  )
)

# 7.b DEA Heatmap Configs
heatmap_config <- list(
  top_n = 30,
  split_by_group = TRUE, 
  row_font_size = 8,
  annotation_vars = c("cohort_group", "Age", "Sex") # <--- Add this!
)

# 7.c GSEA configs (called omics_configs to keep things confusing)
omics_config <- list(
  gsea_p_cutoff  = 0.20, 
  gsea_min_size  = 3,
  network_p_cutoff = 0.1
)
```


# Matrix Factory
`r analysis_decision("Employed three distinct matrix transformations to isolate the disease signature from the Age confounder. The baseline matrix remains unmodified. The pruned matrix surgically removes features correlating heavily with Age (Spearman rho > 0.4). The residualized matrix uses linear modeling to mathematically regress Age out of the coordinate space.")` 

`r what_this_does("Matrix Factory: Dynamically generates custom feature matrices by subsetting the Olink proteomics landscape.", "Transformation: Applies mathematical corrections (Residualization vs. Pruning) to isolate the clinical signature from the Age confounder.", "Audit: Tracks the inclusion/exclusion status of every feature against defined biological thresholds (FDR/FC) to ensure transparency in the dimensionality of your input space.")`

```{r matrix_factory, results='asis'}
# Global storage for the prepared mathematical matrices
prepared_matrices <- list()

for (prep_id in names(matrix_prep_configs)) {
  
  conf <- matrix_prep_configs[[prep_id]]
  res <- wrap_matrix_factory(olink_mat, master_spine, conf, prep_id)
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    prepared_matrices[[prep_id]] <- res$matrix
    
    # 1. Print the raw text log generated by the engine
    cat(res$log_text, "\n\n") 
    
    # 2. Format and Print the Audit Table
    if (!is.null(res$audit_tbl) && nrow(res$audit_tbl) > 0) {
      formatted_tbl <- res$audit_tbl %>%
        mutate(
          # THE FIX: Coerce to numeric first to prevent "unsupported type" logical errors
          logFC = as.numeric(logFC),
          FDR = as.numeric(FDR),
          
          # Clean up the numbers, safely handling NAs
          logFC = if_else(is.na(logFC), "-", as.character(round(logFC, 3))),
          FDR = if_else(is.na(FDR), "-", formatC(FDR, format = "e", digits = 2)),
          
          # Inject HTML color tags for the status
          Final_Status = case_when(
            grepl("Included", Final_Status) ~ sprintf("<span style='color:#27ae60; font-weight:bold;'>%s</span>", Final_Status),
            grepl("Excluded", Final_Status) ~ sprintf("<span style='color:#c0392b; font-weight:bold;'>%s</span>", Final_Status),
            TRUE ~ Final_Status
          )
        ) %>%
        kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE, caption = sprintf("Matrix Audit: %s", prep_id)) %>%
        kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE) %>%
        kableExtra::scroll_box(height = "400px") 
        
      print(formatted_tbl)
    }
    
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("FAILED: %s\n", res$error_msg))
  }
}
```
# PCA {.tabset}
`r what_this_does("Ingested the dynamically pre-processed matrices and performs mean-imputation for complete cases.", "Automatically calculated the mathematical limit of variance (min(N-1, P)) to extract every statistically valid Principal Component.", "Transposed the PCA coordinate space so that Components act as 'Features' for correlation mapping.", "Generated interactive PCA scatterplots colored by requested clinical metadata.")`

```{r PCA, results='asis', fig.width=8, fig.height=8}
pca_results <- list()

for (pca_id in names(pca_configs)) {
  conf <- pca_configs[[pca_id]]
  target_mat <- prepared_matrices[[conf$input_matrix]]
  
  if (is.null(target_mat)) next
  
  # 1. NA Imputation (FactoMineR crashes if ANY NAs exist)
  # Transpose first so patients are rows (required for FactoMineR)
  target_mat_imp <- t(target_mat)
  for(i in 1:ncol(target_mat_imp)) {
    col_mean <- mean(target_mat_imp[, i], na.rm = TRUE)
    target_mat_imp[is.na(target_mat_imp[, i]), i] <- ifelse(is.na(col_mean), 0, col_mean)
  }
  
  # 2. PCA Math
  max_valid_pcs <- min(nrow(target_mat_imp), ncol(target_mat_imp)) - 1
  pca_res <- FactoMineR::PCA(target_mat_imp, scale.unit = TRUE, ncp = max_valid_pcs, graph = FALSE)
  
  # 3. Transpose back for correlation matrix storage
  pca_mat <- t(as.data.frame(pca_res$ind$coord)) 
  pca_results[[pca_id]] <- pca_mat
  
  # Extract the top PCs (or max valid if fewer than 10) 
  n_pcs_to_extract <- min(conf$top_dim, ncol(pca_res$ind$coord))
  # Extract cumulative variance for the selected cutoff
  cum_var <- pca_res$eig[n_pcs_to_extract, 3]
  
  # Store securely in the matrix factory list for downstream wrapper access
  prepared_matrices[[paste0(pca_id, "_top_dims")]] <- t(as.data.frame(pca_res$ind$coord[, 1:n_pcs_to_extract]))
  
  # 4. Visualization (One Tabset Parent per Job)
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n\n", conf$title))
  
  # --- Scree Plot Tab ---
  cat("\n\n### Scree Plot\n\n")
  
  p_scree <- fviz_eig(pca_res, addlabels = TRUE, ylim = c(0, max(pca_res$eig[, 2]) + 10)) +
    theme_project_base() + 
    labs(title = conf$title, subtitle = "Scree Plot: Explained Variance by Component") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = conf$top_dim + 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "firebrick") +
    annotate(
      "text", 
      x = conf$top_dim - 2.7,                  # X position: Slightly to the right of the line
      y = max(pca_res$eig[, 2]) / 2,                          # Y position: High up on the axis
      label = sprintf("Variance included: %.1f%%", cum_var), # %.2f cleans up the decimals
      color = "firebrick", 
      hjust = 0                                # Left-aligns the text so it flows away from the line
    )

  
  print(p_scree)
  cat("\n\n")
  
  # --- NEW: Top Drivers Heatmap Tab ---
  # Dynamically cap at 10 dimensions, or fewer if the matrix is small
  n_dims_to_plot <- min(10, max_valid_pcs)
  
  if (n_dims_to_plot >= 1) {
    cat("\n\n### Top Drivers Heatmap\n\n")
    
    # Extract matrices for the available dimensions
    contrib_mat <- pca_res$var$contrib[, 1:n_dims_to_plot, drop = FALSE]
    cor_mat <- pca_res$var$cor[, 1:n_dims_to_plot, drop = FALSE]
    
    # Find the top 5 driving variables for each dimension based on contribution
    top_vars <- c()
    for (i in 1:n_dims_to_plot) {
      sorted_vars <- rownames(contrib_mat)[order(contrib_mat[, i], decreasing = TRUE)]
      top_vars <- c(top_vars, head(sorted_vars, 5))
    }
    top_vars <- unique(top_vars) # Remove duplicates
    
    # Subset the correlation matrix to only plot these top drivers
    plot_mat <- cor_mat[top_vars, , drop = FALSE]
    
    # Diverging color ramp for Correlation (-1 to 1)
    col_fun <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(-1, 0, 1), c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))
    
    ht <- ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(
      plot_mat,
      name = "Loading",
      column_title = sprintf("Top Drivers across First %d PCs\n(Selected via Contribution)", n_dims_to_plot),
      col = col_fun,
      cluster_columns = FALSE,  # Change to TRUE if PCs should be clustered
      cluster_rows = TRUE,
      show_row_dend = TRUE,
      show_column_dend = TRUE,
      row_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 10),
      column_names_gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "bold"),
      rect_gp = grid::gpar(col = "grey85", lwd = 0.5) # Adds a subtle grid line between cells
    )
    
    ComplexHeatmap::draw(ht)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
  
  # --- Existing: Color Variable Tabs ---
  clin_sub <- master_spine %>% filter(Subject_ID %in% rownames(target_mat_imp))
  
  for (c_var in conf$color_vars) {
    if (!(c_var %in% colnames(clin_sub))) next
    
    cat(sprintf("\n\n### Colored by %s\n\n", c_var))
    
    vec <- clin_sub[[c_var]]
    if (is.numeric(vec)) {
      p <- fviz_pca_ind(pca_res, label = "none", col.ind = vec, legend.title = c_var) +
        scale_color_viridis_c(na.value = "grey85") +
        theme_project_base() + labs(title = conf$title, subtitle = paste("Colored by:", c_var))
    } else {
      p <- fviz_pca_ind(pca_res, label = "none", habillage = as.factor(vec), addEllipses = TRUE) +
        theme_project_base() + labs(title = conf$title, subtitle = paste("Colored by:", c_var))
    }
    print(p)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
}
```

# Algorithm Optimization
`r what_this_does("Executes algorithm-aware hyperparameter sweeps.", "Evaluates Silhouette boundaries for K-Means, PAM, and H-Clust.", "Traces the Noise-to-Cluster tradeoff for HDBSCAN density, and measures Modularity peaks for Louvain network communities.")`

```{r algorithm_optimization}
for (opt_id in names(k_opt_configs)) {
  conf <- k_opt_configs[[opt_id]]
  
  target_matrix <- prepared_matrices[[conf$input_matrix]]
  if (is.null(target_matrix)) next
  
  res <- wrap_k_optimization_pipeline(target_matrix, conf, opt_id, output_dir_data)
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    for (algo in names(res$plots)) {
      print(res$plots[[algo]])
    }
  }
}
```


# Unsupervised Clustering {.tabset}
`r what_this_does("Ingested the dynamically pre-processed matrices from the Matrix Factory.", "Calculated mathematical distances and assigned endotypes using true high-dimensional protein matrices.", "Calculated 2D UMAP coordinates exclusively for visualization, painting high-dimensional labels onto the squashed axes.")`

`r why_is_this_done("Clustering on projected 2D UMAP space introduces manifold distortion. By calculating groupings mathematically across all protein dimensions, we ensure biological robustness, even if the 2D visual projection appears slightly overlapping.")`
```{r unsupervised_clustering, results='asis', fig.height=11, fig.width=10}
# Store the payload globally so the diagnostic chunk can access it later!
global_cluster_payloads <- list()

for (c_id in names(clustering_configs)) {
  conf <- clustering_configs[[c_id]]
  # Inject the Job Title
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n\n", conf$title))
  
  target_matrix <- prepared_matrices[[conf$input_matrix]]
  if (is.null(target_matrix)) next
  
  res <- wrap_clustering_pipeline(target_matrix, master_spine, conf, c_id, output_dir_data, get_project_colors)
  global_cluster_payloads[[c_id]] <- res
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    for (algo in names(res$plots$umaps)) {
      # Inject the Algorithm Tab
     cat(sprintf("\n\n### %s\n\n", toupper(algo)))
     print(res$plots$umaps[[algo]])
      cat("\n\n") # Crucial empty lines for pandoc rendering
    }
  } 
}

cat("\n\n")
```
# Clustering Diagnostic {.tabset}

`r analysis_decision("Employed the Silhouette Score to mathematically quantify algorithm confidence. The algorithm with the highest average width indicates the most robust, well-separated endotypes.")`

`r what_this_does("Extracted the underlying distance matrices and specific patient assignments from the upstream multi-algorithm pipeline.", "Calculated the global Silhouette width for K-Means, PAM, and Hierarchical clustering.", "Generated comparative bar charts to guide algorithmic selection.")`

```{r clustering_diagnostic,results='asis'}
# Store diagnostic results to extract the "best_algo" for Consensus later
global_diagnostic_payloads <- list()

for (job_id in diagnostic_configs$evaluate_jobs) {
  payload <- global_cluster_payloads[[job_id]]
  
  if (!is.null(payload) && payload$status == "success") {
    
    # Extract the exact matrix and config used for this job
    target_matrix <- prepared_matrices[[clustering_configs[[job_id]]$input_matrix]]
    cluster_conf <- clustering_configs[[job_id]]
    
    diag_res <- run_clustering_diagnostics(
      payload, target_matrix, master_spine, data_dictionary, 
      diagnostic_configs, cluster_conf, job_id, output_dir_data
    )
    
    global_diagnostic_payloads[[job_id]] <- diag_res
    
    if (!is.null(diag_res)) {
      # 1. Print the New Global HTML Table
      print(diag_res$plots[["leaderboard"]])
      
      # 2. Print Variable Correlation Plots
      for (v in names(diag_res$plots)) {
        if (v == "leaderboard") next
        print(diag_res$plots[[v]])
      }
    }
  }
}
```

# Clinical Phenotyping {.tabset}
`r analysis_decision("Muted non-significant phenotypes (p >= 0.05) to zero on the heatmap, ensuring visual emphasis is strictly placed on true biological drivers rather than random noise.")`

`r what_this_does("Calculates Log-Odds Ratios and Z-Scores to map the clinical fingerprint of the discovered endotypes. Uses nested categories for streamlined visual profiling.")`

```{r clinical_phenotyping, fig.height=16, fig.width=14, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
for (p_id in names(pheno_configs)) {
  conf <- pheno_configs[[p_id]]
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n\n", conf$title))
  
  payload <- global_cluster_payloads[[conf$target_job]]
  if (is.null(payload)) next
  
  res <- wrap_clinical_phenotyping(payload, master_spine, data_dictionary, conf, p_id, output_dir_data)
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    for (algo in names(res$plots)) {
      # Inject Markdown Header for the Algorithm Tabset
      cat(sprintf("\n\n### %s {.tabset}\n\n", toupper(algo)))
      
      # Loop through nested Heatmaps
      if (length(res$plots[[algo]]$heatmap) > 0) {
        for (h_name in names(res$plots[[algo]]$heatmap)) {
          cat(sprintf("\n\n#### Heatmap: %s\n\n", gsub("_", " ", h_name)))
          ComplexHeatmap::draw(res$plots[[algo]]$heatmap[[h_name]])
          cat("\n\n")
        }
      }
      
      # Loop through nested Highlight Grids
      if (length(res$plots[[algo]]$highlights) > 0) {
        for (g_name in names(res$plots[[algo]]$highlights)) {
          cat(sprintf("\n\n#### Grid: %s\n\n", gsub("_", " ", g_name)))
          print(res$plots[[algo]]$highlights[[g_name]])
          cat("\n\n")
        }
      }
      
       # Print Master Table
      cat("\n\n#### Summary Table\n\n")
      if (!is.null(res$tables[[algo]])) {
        tbl <- res$tables[[algo]] %>% 
          gtsummary::as_kable_extra(caption = sprintf("Clinical Summary: %s", toupper(algo))) %>%
          kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE)%>%
          kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "600px")
        print(tbl)
        cat("\n\n")
      }
    }
  }
}
```

# Feature Phenotyping {.tabset}
`r what_this_does("Runs rigorous Differential Expression Analysis directly between the new, algorithmically discovered endotypes.")`

```{r feature_phenotyping, fig.width=12, fig.height=10, results='asis'}
for (f_id in names(feature_configs)) {
  conf <- feature_configs[[f_id]]
  payload <- global_cluster_payloads[[conf$target_job]]
  target_matrix <- if(conf$input_matrix == "raw") olink_mat else prepared_matrices[[conf$input_matrix]]
  
  if (is.null(payload) || is.null(target_matrix)) next

  # Tier 1: Job Title
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n\n", conf$title))

  for (algo_name in names(conf$algorithms)) {
    # Tier 2: Algorithm Tab
    cat(sprintf("\n\n### %s {.tabset}\n\n", toupper(algo_name)))
    
    # Inject cluster labels for this algorithm
    temp_spine <- master_spine
    temp_spine$Dynamic_Cluster <- as.character(payload$data$assignments[[algo_name]][match(temp_spine$Subject_ID, names(payload$data$assignments[[algo_name]]))])
    temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% filter(!is.na(Dynamic_Cluster), Dynamic_Cluster != "Noise")

    # Tier 3: Contrast Loop
    algo_conf <- conf$algorithms[[algo_name]]
    for (contrast_name in names(algo_conf$contrasts)) {
      c_conf <- algo_conf$contrasts[[contrast_name]]
      
      cat(sprintf("\n\n#### %s\n\n", c_conf$title))
      cat(sprintf("**Description:** %s\n\n", c_conf$desc))

      # Start with the baseline cluster assignments for this algorithm
      contrast_df <- temp_spine

      if (!is.null(c_conf$subset_filter)) {
        filt_col <- c_conf$subset_filter$column
        filt_val <- c_conf$subset_filter$value

        if (filt_col %in% colnames(contrast_df)) {
          # Physically isolate the sub-population BEFORE handing it to Limma
          contrast_df <- contrast_df %>% filter(.data[[filt_col]] %in% filt_val)
        }
      }
      
      # Build the config mapped explicitly to your config naming convention
      dea_conf <- list(
        title = sprintf("%s | Alg: %s", c_conf$title, toupper(algo_name)),
        group_col = "Dynamic_Cluster",
        target_groups = c_conf$target,
        ref_groups = c_conf$ref,
        covariates = conf$covariates,
        dea_p_cutoff = conf$dea_p_cutoff,
        dea_fc_cutoff = conf$dea_fc_cutoff
      )
      
      # Capture the exact job name to use for standardizing image paths
      job_name_full <- sprintf("%s_%s_%s", f_id, algo_name, contrast_name)
      
      # Push the pre-filtered dataframe directly into the core Limma wrapper
      res <- wrap_dea_pipeline(
                target_matrix, 
                contrast_df, 
                dea_conf, 
                job_name_full, 
                output_dir_data, 
                go_genesets, 
                omics_config, 
                heatmap_config, 
                get_project_colors,
                assets_dir = assets_dir
              )
      
      if (res$status == "success") {
        
        # 1. TABLE
        if (nrow(res$tables$significant_proteins) > 0) {
          tbl <- head(res$tables$significant_proteins, 50) %>% 
            mutate(logFC = round(logFC, 2), adj.P.Val = formatC(adj.P.Val, format = "e", digits = 2)) %>%
            kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", caption = "Top 50 Significant Proteins") %>% 
            kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) %>%
            kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "600px")
          print(tbl)
          cat("\n\n") 
        }
        
        # 2. VOLCANO PLOT
        if (!is.null(res$plots$volcano)) {
          if (interactive()) {
            print(res$plots$volcano)
            cat("\n\n")
          } else {
            cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_volcano.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full))
          }
        }
        
        # 3. COMPLEXHEATMAP 
        if (!is.null(res$plots$heatmap)) {
          if (interactive()) {
            ComplexHeatmap::draw(res$plots$heatmap)
            cat("\n\n")
          } else {
            cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_heatmap.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full))
          }
        }
        
        # 4. GSEA PLOTS (Multi-Database Sub-tabs)
        if (length(res$plots$gsea) > 0) {
          cat("\n\n##### Pathway Analysis {.tabset}\n\n")
          
          for (db_name in names(res$plots$gsea)) {
            cat(sprintf("\n\n###### %s\n\n", db_name))
            
            p_obj <- res$plots$gsea[[db_name]]
            
            if (!is.null(p_obj$dotplot)) {
              if (interactive()) { print(p_obj$dotplot); cat("\n\n") }
              else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_%s_dotplot.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full, db_name)) }
            }
            
            if (!is.null(p_obj$ridgeplot)) {
              if (interactive()) { print(p_obj$ridgeplot); cat("\n\n") }
              else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_%s_ridgeplot.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full, db_name)) }
            }
            
            if (!is.null(p_obj$emap)) {
              if (interactive()) { print(p_obj$emap); cat("\n\n") }
              else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_%s_emap.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full, db_name)) }
            }
          }
        } else {
          cat("*Note: No pathways passed FDR for any database.* \n\n")
        }
        
        # 5. LEGACY GSEA RIDGEPLOT
        if (!is.null(res$plots$gsea_ridge)) {
          if (interactive()) { print(res$plots$gsea_ridge); cat("\n\n") }
          else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_gsea_ridge.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full)) }
        }
        
        # 6. LEGACY GSEA EMAP PLOT
        if (!is.null(res$plots$gsea_emap)) {
          if (interactive()) { print(res$plots$gsea_emap); cat("\n\n") }
          else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/03c_proteomics_clustering/%s_gsea_emap.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", job_name_full)) }
        }
      }
    }
    cat("\n\n")
  }
}
```

<!-- # Consensus Export -->

<!-- `r analysis_decision("Utilized a Reference-Based Overlap algorithm to translate arbitrary cluster numbers (e.g. Cluster 1 vs Cluster 3) into a unified coordinate space.", "Automatically anchored the alignment matrix to the algorithm with the highest average Silhouette confidence.")` -->

<!-- ` r what_this_does("Evaluated row-wise mode to determine the Final Consensus Endotype for every patient.", "Assigned patients failing the stability threshold into a 'Mixed/Transitional' category.", "Exported a comprehensive .rds file containing translated sub-clusters, stability scores, and individual silhouette scores for downstream multi-omics integration.")` -->

<!-- ```{r consensus_export} -->
<!-- for (export_id in names(export_configs)) { -->
<!--   conf <- export_configs[[export_id]] -->

<!--   target_payload <- global_cluster_payloads[[conf$target_job]] -->
<!--   target_diag <- global_diagnostic_payloads[[conf$target_job]] -->

<!--   if (is.null(target_payload) || is.null(target_diag)) next -->

<!--   # Auto-select anchor if requested -->
<!--   anchor <- if (conf$anchor_algorithm == "auto") target_diag$best_algo else conf$anchor_algorithm -->

<!--   res <- wrap_consensus_export(target_payload, anchor, conf, export_id, output_dir_data) -->

<!--   if (res$status == "success") { -->
<!--     # Print the raw text log natively -->
<!--     cat(res$text_log, "\n") -->

<!--     # Render the new diagnostics dynamically to the RStudio Viewer -->
<!--     print(res$plots$convergence_bar) -->
<!--     print(res$plots$consensus_umap) -->
<!--   } -->
<!-- } -->
<!-- ``` -->


# Appendix
`r what_this_does("Appended the raw underlying R code from the custom predictive engines to the HTML report's appendix to ensure complete analytical transparency and reproducibility.")`
```{r print_engine, code=c(readLines(here::here("code", "01_general_engines.R")), readLines(here::here("code", "02_proteomics_engines.R"))), eval=FALSE}

```

```{r complete}
cat("Script 03c Complete! \n")
```

```{r legacy, include=FALSE}
# Legacy Clin Config ----  
  # pheno_svc_act = list(
  #   target_job = "job_svc",
  #   title = "Age Regressed AvC DEA PCA Clustering Active only",
  #   target_algorithms = c("kmeans"),
  #   subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Active")),
  #   variables = clin_vars_to_include,
  #   highlight_variables = clin_vars_to_highlight ,
  #   p_cutoff = 0.05,
  #   must_include_vars = c("cohort_group")
  # ),
  # 
  # pheno_svc_nonact = list(
  #   target_job = "job_svc",
  #   title = "Age Regressed AvC DEA PCA Clustering Nonactive only",
  #   target_algorithms = c("kmeans"),
  #   subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Non-Active")),
  #   variables = clin_vars_to_include,
  #   highlight_variables = clin_vars_to_highlight ,
  #   p_cutoff = 0.05,
  #   must_include_vars = c("cohort_group")
  # )
  
# Legacy Feature Config ----
  # feat_svc_act = list(
  #   target_job = "job_svc",
  #   title = "Age Regressed SvC DEA Clustering Active only",
  #   input_matrix = "raw", 
  #   covariates = c("Age"),
  #   # Algorithms are now the primary key to enable contrast-per-algorithm
  #   algorithms = list(
  #     kmeans = list(
  #       contrasts = list(
  #         c1_vs_c2 = list(title = "Cluster 1 vs Cluster 2 Active only", desc = "Both mixed active and nonacctive", 
  #                         target=c("Cluster 1"), ref=c("Cluster 2"),
  #                         subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Active")))
  #       )
  #     )
  #   ),
  #   dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,
  #   dea_fc_cutoff = 0.58
  # ),
  # 
  # feat_svc_nonact = list(
  #   target_job = "job_svc",
  #   title = "Age Regressed SvC DEA Clustering Nonactive only",
  #   input_matrix = "raw", 
  #   covariates = c("Age"),
  #   # Algorithms are now the primary key to enable contrast-per-algorithm
  #   algorithms = list(
  #     kmeans = list(
  #       contrasts = list(
  #         c1_vs_c2 = list(title = "Cluster 1 vs Cluster 2 Nonactive only", desc = "Both mixed active and nonacctive", 
  #                         target=c("Cluster 1"), ref=c("Cluster 2"),
  #                         subset_filter = list(column = "cohort_group", value = c("Non-Active")))
  #       )
  #     )
  #   ),
  #   dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,
  #   dea_fc_cutoff = 0.58
  # )
```
